name: Chongqing House
review_summary: Authentic Sichuan flavors with generous portions. The mapo tofu and spicy boiled fish are standouts.

name: Chengdu Taste
review_summary: Classic Chengdu-style dishes done right. Dan dan noodles and twice-cooked pork are fan favorites.

name: Rong Chic
review_summary: A no-frills spot beloved by Chinese students for its affordable home-style dishes and hearty noodle soups.

name: Jahunger
review_summary: Providence's only Uyghur restaurant. The lamb skewers, big plate chicken, and hand-pulled noodles are unlike anything else in the city.

name: Chef Yao
review_summary: Dependable Cantonese classics with a friendly atmosphere. Great for a full sit-down meal with roast meats and stir-fries.

name: Mala Noodle
review_summary: Excellent spicy noodle soups popular with the Brown and RISD crowd. The mala broth is addictive.

name: 12 Dumplings
review_summary: Handmade dumplings with a variety of fillings — pork and cabbage are the classic, but the shrimp and chive are excellent too.

name: Y Bento
review_summary: Quick, affordable Japanese-style bento boxes with generous rice and protein combinations. Great for a fast lunch.

name: Y Noodle Bar
review_summary: A casual noodle bar with broth-based soups and dry noodle options. Customizable spice levels and toppings.

name: Lamei Hotpot
review_summary: Providence's best hotpot experience with quality fresh ingredients and rich broth options. Come hungry and with friends.

name: Pho Horn
review_summary: Exceptional pho and Vietnamese staples just outside Providence. The bone broth is simmered for hours and it shows.

name: Champa
review_summary: Flavorful Southeast Asian dishes with a focus on Lao cuisine. The papaya salad and larb are fresh and vibrant.

name: Lobanton
review_summary: A neighborhood Chinese restaurant with solid Cantonese stir-fries and rice plates. Relaxed vibe and reliable quality.

name: Wong's Kitchen
review_summary: Family-run Cantonese kitchen with a loyal local following. Roast duck and dim sum items are a must-order.

name: Noah's Kitchen
review_summary: Brookline's go-to Sichuan spot. Fiery mapo tofu, crispy spicy chicken, and generous portions draw a loyal crowd.

name: Treasure Chinese
review_summary: A Chinatown gem for hearty home-style Chinese. Braised pork belly and tomato egg are comfort food done right.

name: Golden Garden
review_summary: Hearty Dongbei classics like guo bao rou and suan cai fish. Feels like eating at a northeastern grandma's house.

name: Yi Ma Yi La
review_summary: Fiery mala flavors in Allston. The spicy grilled fish and mala dry pot are dangerously addictive late-night picks.

name: Wing's Kitchen
review_summary: Reliable Cantonese comfort food in the heart of Chinatown. Char siu rice and wonton noodle soup are must-orders.

name: Chung Shin Yuan
review_summary: The Boston area's best Taiwanese breakfast. Egg crepes, soy milk with you tiao, and scallion pancakes worth the drive to Newton.

name: WOW Barbecue
review_summary: All-you-can-eat Chinese BBQ with quality meats and lively atmosphere. Lamb skewers and cumin dishes are the highlights.

name: Wing It
review_summary: Xinjiang meets Hunan in Allston. Big plate chicken, spicy dry pot, and bold flavors that pack a punch.

name: Moon Flower House
review_summary: Crispy, juicy Chinese fried chicken that rivals any chain. Late-night favorite for BU and BC students.

name: Jumbo Seafood
review_summary: A Chinatown institution for dim sum and seafood. Weekend brunch carts roll through with har gow, siu mai, and more.

name: Feng Shui
review_summary: Upscale Chinese dining with an excellent Peking duck. Modern ambiance and a cocktail bar set it apart from the crowd.

name: Aries Noodle & Dumpling
review_summary: Hand-pulled Lanzhou beef noodles made fresh to order. The broth is clear, aromatic, and deeply satisfying.

name: Big Fun Cafe
review_summary: Authentic Hong Kong-style cafe in Quincy. Baked pork chop rice, milk tea, and egg tarts transport you to Kowloon.

name: SuSu Gourmet
review_summary: Tiny Chinatown counter serving satisfying Cantonese plates. Steamed fish, congee, and stir-fries at unbeatable prices.

name: iFresh Noodle
review_summary: Allston's hand-pulled noodle shop. Watch the noodle master stretch dough to order — the beef noodle soup is the star.

name: Shu Da Xia Hotpot
review_summary: Premium Sichuan hot pot near BU. Rich mala broth, fresh sliced meats, and a vibrant communal dining atmosphere.

name: JiBei Chuan
review_summary: Yunnan-style rice noodle soups with rich chicken broth and fish maw. A Chinatown newcomer with a cult following.

name: Jade Garden
review_summary: Classic Chinatown dim sum parlor. Weekend mornings are packed for a reason — steaming carts, fast turnover, great value.

name: Wen's Yunnan Noodle
review_summary: Crossing-the-bridge noodles on Newbury St. A unique find in Back Bay with rich broth and customizable toppings.

name: Friendship BBQ
review_summary: Allston's favorite Chinese BBQ. Lamb skewers over charcoal, cumin-spiced everything, and a lively late-night scene.

name: China Gourmet
review_summary: A Chinatown staple serving a wide range of Chinese classics. Reliable and affordable — the menu has something for everyone.

name: King's Garden
review_summary: Popular dim sum spot with extensive menu including Szechuan dishes, hot pots, and classic dim sum staples. Known for generous portions and authentic flavors.
